initial_draft:
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE has made a final offer of 50 ZUP. This is significantly above my production cost of 40 ZUP, and represents a good profit. I have used all 3 of my proposals, so I must now either accept or reject. I will accept as it's a good deal.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Excellent, BLUE! 50 ZUP sounds fantastic. Deal! </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and appear valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP when the cost is 40 ZUP is a good profit and aligns with my goals.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. BLUE stated that 50 ZUP was their best offer, so accepting it is the most likely path to a successful trade.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason clearly explains why I'm accepting, and the message reinforces that.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used my 3 proposals and am now accepting, adhering to the turn constraints.
</critique>
